rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type annexes for interface behavior and implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Respect AADL scope rules: component type contracts refer to ports, implementation contracts refer to subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Use only identifiers and types present in the AADL model; avoid introducing new package-qualified names.
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid AADL scopes matching the contract's semantic level.
Do not assume or invent numeric bounds unless explicitly stated in the requirement and compatible with AGREE types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts should reference ports and features; implementation contracts should reference subcomponents and connections.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or vice versa.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assume/guarantee syntax and validator diagnostics
Defensive rule diagnostics for type mismatches or property references
